preview_text:
The model rates Mexico as favorite with a 49% win probability, roughly double South Korea's 21% chance. The draw emerges as a secondary outcome at 30%, suggesting the match has meaningful uncertainty despite the tilted odds.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a competitive but asymmetric contest. Mexico holds a clear edge, yet the substantial draw probability indicates neither side is heavily favored to settle the matter in regular time. South Korea retains enough upside to avoid being a heavy underdog, making this more than a one-sided affair.

Model and sportsbook are closely aligned. Mexico sits at 49% versus 51%, the draw at 30% versus 27%, and South Korea at 21% versus 22%. These gaps fall within normal variance across forecast sources. No Polymarket data is available yet.
